_description: Workflow stage definitions for each Recovery task type. Each type has its own stage progression reflecting the actual work pattern. Stages map to the 'stage' field in task metadata — NOT the generic 'status' field, which stays synced (e.g., stage='negotiation' → status='in-progress').

status_mapping._description: Maps stage categories to the existing Mission Control status values for Kanban board compatibility.

agreement._description: Sales/agreement pipeline. Mirrors a B2B sales cycle from lead identification to close.

stages.label: Identification
stages.description: Potential agreement opportunity identified. Needs initial research and qualification.

stages.label: Qualification
stages.description: Validating the opportunity is real — budget, authority, need, timeline (BANT).

stages.label: Outreach
stages.description: Active engagement — calls, emails, meetings to build the relationship and pitch.

stages.label: Proposal
stages.description: Formal proposal or SOW delivered. Awaiting review.

stages.label: Negotiation
stages.description: Active negotiation on terms, pricing, scope. Agreement-maker leads.

stages.label: Closing
stages.description: Final contract review, signatures, payment setup.

stages.label: Won
stages.description: Agreement signed and active. Trigger onboarding and retention handoff.

stages.label: Lost
stages.description: Opportunity did not close. Log reason and schedule future re-engagement.

partnership._description: Partnership development lifecycle. Longer cycle, more collaborative, less transactional than agreements.

stages.label: Discovery
stages.description: Identifying potential partners and assessing strategic fit.

stages.label: Relationship Building
stages.description: Building trust and rapport. Multiple touchpoints before formalizing.

stages.label: Value Alignment
stages.description: Defining mutual value proposition. What does each side contribute and receive?

stages.label: Formalization
stages.description: Creating formal partnership terms, MOUs, or agreements.

stages.label: Activation
stages.description: Launching the partnership. Integrations, co-marketing, referral setup.

stages.label: Active
stages.description: Partnership is live and generating value. Transitions to keeper for ongoing management.

stages.label: Dormant
stages.description: Partnership inactive but not terminated. May reactivate.

stages.label: Terminated
stages.description: Partnership formally ended.

retention._description: Account retention workflow. Triggered by risk signals or scheduled reviews. Fast-moving — retention is time-critical.

stages.label: Alert
stages.description: Risk signal detected. Needs immediate triage.

stages.label: Diagnosis
stages.description: Understanding the root cause of the retention risk.

stages.label: Intervention
stages.description: Executing the retention play — outreach, offer, escalation, or remediation.

stages.label: Resolution
stages.description: Determining outcome — did the intervention work?

stages.label: Retained
stages.description: Account saved. Schedule follow-up check.

stages.label: Churned
stages.description: Account lost. Log learnings and schedule win-back attempt.

pr._description: Public relations workflow. Varies by PR type — crisis is fast-tracked, proactive campaigns follow full pipeline.

stages.label: Ideation
stages.description: PR opportunity or need identified. Brainstorming angle and approach.

stages.label: Content Creation
stages.description: Writing press releases, pitches, talking points, or content pieces.

stages.label: Approval
stages.description: Content review and sign-off before external distribution.
exit_criteria: approved by Recovery lead or Sean

stages.label: Media Outreach
stages.description: Pitching to journalists, influencers, and media contacts.

stages.label: Coverage Tracking
stages.description: Monitoring for published coverage and social amplification.
exit_criteria: coverage_results populated OR 30-day tracking period elapsed

stages.label: Complete
stages.description: PR campaign concluded. Results logged for effectiveness analysis.

fast_track_rules._description: Crisis PR skips ideation and approval stages for speed.
